original_query: select p_brand, p_type, p_size, count(distinct ps_suppkey) as supplier_cnt from partsupp, part where p_partkey = ps_partkey and p_brand <> 'Brand#43' and p_type not like 'PROMO PLATED%' and p_size in (18, 8, 33, 17, 27, 6, 1, 50) and ps_suppkey not in ( select s_suppkey from supplier where s_comment like '%Customer%Complaints%' ) group by p_brand, p_type, p_size order by supplier_cnt desc, p_brand, p_type, p_size;
rewritten_query:
SELECT p_brand, p_type, p_size, COUNT(DISTINCT ps_suppkey) AS supplier_cnt
FROM partsupp
JOIN part ON p_partkey = ps_partkey
WHERE p_brand <> 'Brand#43'
  AND p_type NOT LIKE 'PROMO PLATED%'
  AND p_size IN (18, 8, 33, 17, 27, 6, 1, 50)
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM supplier
    WHERE supplier.s_suppkey = partsupp.ps_suppkey
      AND supplier.s_comment LIKE '%Customer%Complaints%'
  )
GROUP BY p_brand, p_type, p_size
ORDER BY supplier_cnt DESC, p_brand, p_type, p_size;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Replace NOT IN subquery with NOT EXISTS for better performance. The NOT EXISTS correlated subquery can allow the optimizer to use more efficient anti-join strategies and avoid issues with NULL values.
Use explicit JOIN syntax instead of implicit comma joins for better readability and to help the optimizer make better decisions about join order and methods.

original_query: select p_brand, p_type, p_size, count(distinct ps_suppkey) as supplier_cnt from partsupp, part where p_partkey = ps_partkey and p_brand <> 'Brand#43' and p_type not like 'MEDIUM BURNISHED%' and p_size in (48, 45, 42, 31, 44, 33, 50, 3) and ps_suppkey not in ( select s_suppkey from supplier where s_comment like '%Customer%Complaints%' ) group by p_brand, p_type, p_size order by supplier_cnt desc, p_brand, p_type, p_size;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_suppliers AS (
    SELECT s_suppkey
    FROM supplier
    WHERE s_comment LIKE '%Customer%Complaints%'
)
SELECT 
    p.p_brand,
    p.p_type,
    p.p_size,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ps.ps_suppkey) AS supplier_cnt
FROM 
    part AS p
INNER JOIN 
    partsupp AS ps ON p.p_partkey = ps.ps_partkey
LEFT JOIN 
    filtered_suppliers fs ON ps.ps_suppkey = fs.s_suppkey
WHERE 
    p.p_brand <> 'Brand#43'
    AND p.p_type NOT LIKE 'MEDIUM BURNISHED%'
    AND p.p_size IN (48, 45, 42, 31, 44, 33, 50, 3)
    AND fs.s_suppkey IS NULL
GROUP BY 
    p.p_brand,
    p.p_type,
    p.p_size
ORDER BY 
    supplier_cnt DESC,
    p.p_brand,
    p.p_type,
    p.p_size;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Use explicit INNER JOIN syntax instead of implicit joins (comma-separated FROM clause) for better readability and maintainability.
Add table aliases (p for part, ps for partsupp, s for supplier) to improve query readability and clarity.

original_query: select p_brand, p_type, p_size, count(distinct ps_suppkey) as supplier_cnt from partsupp, part where p_partkey = ps_partkey and p_brand <> 'Brand#33' and p_type not like 'SMALL BRUSHED%' and p_size in (28, 8, 22, 14, 19, 44, 47, 45) and ps_suppkey not in ( select s_suppkey from supplier where s_comment like '%Customer%Complaints%' ) group by p_brand, p_type, p_size order by supplier_cnt desc, p_brand, p_type, p_size;
rewritten_query: select p_brand, p_type, p_size, count(distinct ps_suppkey) as supplier_cnt from partsupp, part where p_partkey = ps_partkey and p_brand <> 'Brand#33' and p_type not like 'SMALL BRUSHED%' and p_size in (28, 8, 22, 14, 19, 44, 47, 45) and ps_suppkey not in ( select s_suppkey from supplier where s_comment like '%Customer%Complaints%' ) group by p_brand, p_type, p_size order by supplier_cnt desc, p_brand, p_type, p_size;
